chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Cr. Acctt. Appeal No.01 of 2026 Date Order with signature of the Judge Present: Mr. Justice Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro. Mr. Justice Khalid Hussain Shahani. Haji Adam Khan Jokhio..............Vs........................ The State 14.04.2026. Mr. Qamar Abbas Abbasi, Advocate for appellant Syed Manzoor Ali, Special Prosecutor NAB a/w Wasif Khan AD/I.O. O R D E R = MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO J: Appellant has filed appeal against the impugned order dated 07.01.2025, whereby learned Accountability Court-V has endorsed freezing by the NAB of the properties of the appellant i.e. Land admeasuring 159 acres vide entry/Chalta No.1, Property Register of Deh K-28 in the name of M/s Ari S/o Haji Meeran & 68 others and 160 acres land vide Entry/Chalta No.1, Property Register of Deh K-28 in the name of M/s Usman S/o Sadiq & others. The contention raised by appellant through his counsel is that this enquiry has been pending since 2019 and has not yet been concluded, meanwhile through the impugned order, freezing of the properties of the appellant has been confirmed. 2. Learned Special Prosecutor NAB and Enquiry Officer are present. Enquiry Officer submits that within a month or so, the enquiry report will be submitted before the Board for a decision. 3. We have seen the impugned order and considered the pleas. Without observing anything on merits, as the enquiry is pending, we give 1 1⁄2 months’ time to the Enquiry Officer to submit enquiry report before the Board and in case any adverse observation or decision is made, the appellant would be at liberty to challenge the same before the proper forum.

We have seen the impugned order and considered the pleas. Without observing anything on merits, as the enquiry is pending, we give 1 1⁄2 months’ time to the Enquiry Officer to submit enquiry report before the Board and in case any adverse observation or decision is made, the appellant would be at liberty to challenge the same before the proper forum. However, this appeal, premature as it is, keeping in view the pendency of the enquiry, is dismissed in the circumstances. The Cr. Appeal is disposed of. JUDGE JUDGE A.K
